records.ety: [F. crucifix or LL. crucifixum, fr. L. crux, crucis, cross + figere, fixum, to fix. See Cross, and Fix, and cf. Crucify.]

defs: A representation in art of the figure of Christ upon the cross; esp., the sculptured figure affixed to a real cross of wood, ivory, metal, or the like, used by the Roman Catholics in their devotions.
quotes: The cross, too, by degrees, become the crucifix.
And kissing oft her crucifix, Unto the block she drew.

defs: The cross or religion of Christ.
